title: https.request with rejectUnauthorized: false — TLS certificate validation disabled (MITM)

params.passDetail: https.request keeps certificate validation on (rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the server's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: https.request is called with rejectUnauthorized: false, which turns OFF TLS certificate validation. Any machine on the network path can present a self-signed certificate and silently man-in-the-middle the connection — credentials and data are exposed. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; if a private CA is needed, pass `ca` instead.
params.absentCallDetail: No https.request call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: https.request does not set rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (true) applies. No action needed.

provenance.note: rejectUnauthorized: false is a notorious 'make the TLS error go away' fix that ships to production and silently disables MITM protection. Documented under the TLS options https.request accepts.
